mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiple expansion, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is not accelerating sharply.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling over the prior 1-3 months, or if the signal reverses during periods of stable or rising CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above the prior 3-month level signals weakening household income expectations and recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances of rising UNRATE do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears when PAYEMS is simultaneously expanding.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand strength in consumption and housing, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint expansions in RSAFS and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising faster than its recent trend while CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, the market reads improving real activity without an inflation shock, which supports near-term S&P 500 gains.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.0% over 2 months and CPIAUCSL is flat or down over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not improve 20-day SPX returns versus baseline, or if positive industrial production growth accompanied by accelerating CPIAUCSL leads to negative returns instead.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in GDPC1 signals weakening aggregate demand and lower expected corporate revenue growth, which should weigh on S&P 500 performance over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly declines in GDPC1 are not followed by lower 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign is positive during repeated GDP contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in FEDFUNDS above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if high-rate episodes coincide with strong positive equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS accelerates while UNRATE falls, labor income and confidence improve together, supporting consumer demand and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 0.2% over 2 months and UNRATE falls over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll growth and falling unemployment do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect remains after removing one of the two labor variables.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior trough signals renewed housing demand and stronger downstream spending, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next week as the market prices cyclical improvement quickly.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% from its prior 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound events are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the signal only works after broader GDP or payroll improvements are already present.
